'''Sand Worms''' or '''sandworms''' were rare creatures that lived deep in their burrowed holes in the desert sands of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. They traveled very fast underground and were hard to catch in a fight,<ref name="Super" /> and were a favorite food of the [[Krayt dragon/Legends|Krayt dragon]].<ref name="Secrets">''[[Super Star Wars Official Game Secrets]]''</ref>

[[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] [[Beastmaster/Legends|beast master]] [[Jai Quallo]] of the Tatooine [[Dust Vipers]] gang had a sand worm among his pet beasts, who were trained to assist him in a fight.<ref name="KOTFE">{{TORcite|exp=kotet|type=flashpoint|Uprising: Done and Dusted}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ancient_Sand_Worm.jpg|thumb|left|Ancient Sand Worm in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'']]
The Tatooine sand worm first appeared as an enemy in [[1992]] ''[[Super Star Wars]]'' video game and was also featured in the ''[[Super Star Wars Official Game Secrets]]'' guide released next year. Depicted as a long, burrowing snake-like creature with pink skin and green eyes,<ref name="Super" /> the sand worm received little further mentions, like most original elements from that game.

The [[2011]] MMORPG ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' later featured a single creature named "Ancient Sand Worm" in the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. This Sand Worm is depicted as a tall, eyeless [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]]-looking creature, and in fact uses the same in-game model as much smaller [[Killik/Legends|killik]] larvae.<ref name="SWTOR" /> [[2016]] expansion [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Eternal Throne|''Knights of the Eternal Throne'']] featured this version of Sand Worms more prominently in the "Done and Dusted" uprising, clearly using the name "Sand Worm" for the creatures, as well as related achievement and in-game housing decoration.<ref name="KOTFE" />
